title: Filter Schoology Messages in Gmail
desc: Use this to filter and move emails from Schoology to clean up your inbox and find those emails easily
author: Jon F.
text: ⌂ All guides 🔗 Copy link ↗ Open in new tab ⬇ PDF Filter Schoology Messages in Gmail Use this to filter and move emails from Schoology to clean up your inbox and find those emails easily ✍️ Made by Jon F. 1 Select an email from Schoology (Click the check box for that email)

title: Change Schoology Notifications Settings
desc: This will help you change the Notifications Settings so Schoology does not send them to your email
author: Jon F.
text: ⌂ All guides 🔗 Copy link ↗ Open in new tab ⬇ PDF Change Schoology Notifications Settings This will help you change the Notifications Settings so Schoology does not send them to your email ✍️ Made by Jon F. 1 Click the Account button

title: Import a Test/Quiz File to Schoology & Create and Utilize Question Banks
text: 📖 Chapters 📖 Chapters Uploading Test/Quiz File to Schoology Uploading a Question Bank for Test/Quiz (for Assessments) Using a Question Bank to Create a Randomized Test/Quiz (Green) ⌂ All guides 🔗 Copy link ↗ Open in new tab Import a Test/Quiz File to Schoology & Create and Utilize Question Banks By JONATHAN FROELICH Uploading Test/Quiz File to Schoology ⚠️ Alert! This is how you will upload the exported file created from the QuizPro Tool linked here: https://sites.google.com/apps.isd742.org/quizextractor/home 1 Head to your Schoology course 2 Click "Add Materials" then "Add Test/Quiz"

title: Using the Quiz Converter Tool
text: ⌂ All guides 🔗 Copy link ↗ Open in new tab Using the Quiz Converter Tool By JONATHAN FROELICH 1 Navigate to https://sites.google.com/apps.isd742.org/quizextractor/home Select the Gem link that suits your needs best The left button will take an existing test you have (pdf, word doc etc) and convert it to code for the tool. All you need to do is upload your test and hit the submit button. The right option creates questions from scratch (Example: make me 50 questions about topic X, you can upload slide shows, documents, etc to use to create these questions)

title: How To Clean up Your Gmail
desc: Learn how to efficiently manage your inbox by moving large volumes of messages at once. This guide will help you streamline your workflow and keep your email organized with ease.
text: ⌂ All guides 🔗 Copy link ↗ Open in new tab How To Clean up Your Gmail Learn how to efficiently manage your inbox by moving large volumes of messages at once. This guide will help you streamline your workflow and keep your email organized with ease. 1 Step 1: Head to your Gmail inbox 2 Click the "select" button near the top left corner as shown Alternatively you can search for specific emails you want to save. Find those emails (by sender, date, etc) and follow the same directions below starting on Step 4.
